_comment_top: Example dispatch config for opencode_dispatch.py. Copy to <project>/.colosseum/dispatch.json and edit. Schema described in colosseum/scripts/opencode_dispatch.py.
_comment_voices: Voice roster. id is a filesystem-safe slug used in output paths and headers. model is the OpenCode model string. burnt/* routes through the gateway; lmstudio/* routes through local LM Studio. Add or remove voices to match your panel. For routine work, 2-3 voices including one local; for high-stakes milestones, 5-7 voices spanning multiple families.

voices.note: Moonshot frontier tier; clean sequential ReAct produces 6.8-15K-char per-slice reports
Anthropic via gateway; for the Claude voice in a multi-model ensemble, prefer the Agent subagent (Mode 2). This entry only makes sense in a cross-harness manifest run.
Google local 26B; needs ≥30min per-call timeout for ReAct loops on consumer GPU

_comment_excluded_voices: Voices known to fail adversarial spec review: glm-4-7-flash (degenerate loops at 30B 'flash' tier); goedel-prover-v2-32b and other theorem-prover specialists (treat prompts as proof goals instead of attack targets — use at verify-pyramid layer instead).
_comment_slices: Slice plan. Each slice is one (voice, slice) call. headers[] are the markdown header ranges the agent should read from the target spec. attack_emphasis is the per-slice attack-category hint. Replace this example with your project's actual sections.

slices.name: scope
slices.label: System identity, non-goals, external contracts
slices.attack_emphasis: Identity overreach; non-goals that should be in scope; caller-contract clauses that admit clients the doc intends to exclude.

slices.name: behaviors-core
slices.label: Worked examples — happy path, boundary, edge, explicit failures
slices.attack_emphasis: Worked-example arithmetic; edge-case completeness; explicit-failure listing completeness; happy-path implicit assumptions.

slices.name: invariants
slices.label: Structural and behavioral invariants
slices.attack_emphasis: Pointwise evaluability of state invariants; temporal-tag correctness; coverage gaps; derived-flag soundness.

_comment_context_appendix: Optional shared-context block prepended to every call. Use for type signatures, invariant labels, or cross-section glossary entries that are load-bearing but not under attack. Mark clearly as read-only / not-to-be-attacked.
context_appendix:
=== CONTEXT APPENDIX (read-only — DO NOT ATTACK; these sections are attacked in their own invocations) ===

System under review: <your system name and one-line description>.
Intent doc: version <X.Y.Z>.

=== END CONTEXT APPENDIX ===
_comment_timeouts: per_call_timeout: seconds budget per (voice, slice) call. Local reasoning models on consumer hardware can take 10-25 min per slice. max_retries: per-call retry budget on truncated stubs or HTTP errors.
